_: Canonical content for /pitch. Edit here, not in components. Per-audience overrides under `audiences.{key}.overrides`. Numbers verified against tagrides-pitch-deck.pdf + survey CSVs (210 responses) + Lagos route research (Nov-2024).

customer.label: Customer
customer.tagline: For riders and drivers who want to know what TagRides actually does for them.

cover.title: Their route. Your ride.
cover.subtitle: Built first in Lagos for everyone daily ride-hail leaves behind. Drivers heading your way pick you up, you agree the fare directly, and you pay only for your leg of the trip.

problem.title: Lagos commutes punish everyone — and reward no one.
problem.subtitle: Riders overpay or suffer. Drivers waste empty seats. The same math punishes every emerging-market megacity. We're starting in Lagos because we know its streets.

personas.name: Fatima
pain: Pays ₦4,500–₦7,400 daily for Bolt to feel safe — 6–10× what a danfo costs.
Loses 30+ hours a week to traffic and stop-and-go pickups.
Doesn't trust danfos: no records, no accountability, no comfort.

personas.name: Emeka
pain: Drives Lagos roads daily — 3 empty seats every trip.
Bolt detours kill his schedule and his fuel margin.
No way to earn from the route he's already taking.

lagosCost.eyebrow: WHAT IT COSTS TO MOVE IN LAGOS
lagosCost.title: Ride-hailing is 3–10× more expensive than the alternative.
lagosCost.subtitle: 30 routes researched, Nov 2024. Same start. Same destination. Same time of day. The gap is the market.

stats.label: Average time a Lagosian spends in traffic
Cars per kilometer in Lagos (global avg: 11)
Estimated annual productivity loss from Lagos congestion
Approx. share of the world priced out of daily private ride-hail

lagosCost.source: TagRides field research — 30 Lagos routes, n=30 respondents, Nov 2024.

solution.title: Tag along the routes drivers already drive.
solution.subtitle: Drivers stay on their route. Riders walk to a parked driver or to a pickup point on a moving driver's route.

mechanics.title: Share the trip
mechanics.detail: An Along driver enters a destination and available seats. TagRides maps the route and works out the pickup.

mechanics.title: Send separate offers
mechanics.detail: TagRides drafts a fare for the road shared with each driver. The rider can edit and send several offers; each driver accepts or counters once.

mechanics.title: Start with the first match
mechanics.detail: TagRides makes the first agreed match the pickup automatically. The rider can switch to another accepted driver until boarding confirms the seat and payment.

product.title: Built in Flutter. Live on AWS. Already running.
product.subtitle: The full rider flow, end to end, in one app — already in the hands of internal testers across Lagos.

market.title: A global market hidden in journeys already happening.
market.subtitle: TagRides fills empty seats in cars, taxis, vans, minibuses, tricycles, and motorcycles where lawful, without creating a new driver trip. Lagos is the first launch market, not the limit.

facts.label: Illustrative daily rides across five cities
Assumed average rider fare in the five-city case

market.source: TagRides scenario model. Planning assumptions, not a third-party market forecast.

model.title: Earn when a rider fills an empty seat.
model.subtitle: Along does not pay drivers to approach. If a pickup fails, the driver stays on the original route. TagRides earns only when the rider boards.

streams.label: Commission on rider fares
streams.detail: Charged only when the rider boards. Driver and rider both see the fee up-front.

streams.label: Platform services
streams.detail: Route intelligence, driver verification, and fare-estimate tools for logistics, fintech, and mobility partners under TAG-ALONG LTD.

pricing.label: Distance-based base
pricing.detail: Per-km tariff visible before the rider taps Request.

pricing.label: Time-of-day modifier
pricing.detail: Higher during rush — but capped at the driver's accepted offer, never algorithmic surge.

pricing.label: One-round fare decision
pricing.detail: The rider sends the system's draft. The driver accepts or counters once. The rider accepts, declines, or leaves the response open.

gtm.title: Win one Lagos corridor first. Then scale the template.
gtm.subtitle: We don't try to be everywhere on day one. We pick the highest-density commute in Lagos, saturate it, prove unit economics, then unlock the next corridor.

lanes.title: Wedge
lanes.detail: Own the Yaba ↔ Lagos Island corridor — the iconic Mainland-to-Island commute. UNILAG, YabaTech, Computer Village, Sabo, Adekunle, Onike all feed into Marina across Eko Bridge.
tactics: Field marketing at UNILAG main gate, Sabo, Jibowu, Adekunle, Iddo, Marina
Campus partnerships with UNILAG + YabaTech student bodies

lanes.title: Supply
lanes.detail: 300 verified drivers and route operators already travelling daily Yaba ↔ Island routes.
tactics: Recruit commuters, taxis, vans, and existing route operators on the Mainland-Island axis
Driver community ops (WhatsApp groups, weekly meetups)
Partner with vehicle inspection + insurance providers

lanes.title: Demand
lanes.detail: 20,000 monthly active riders along the Yaba commute belt.
tactics: Referral economy (₦500 credit per converted invite)
Content SEO targeting Lagos commute queries (Yaba → CMS, Sabo → Marina etc)

lanes.title: Expansion
lanes.detail: Lagos-wide → Abuja → Accra / Nairobi / Kigali. Then cities where daily private ride-hail prices out the working class — SE Asia, LATAM, the same corridor playbook ports.
tactics: Local co-founders / city leads in each new market
Open APIs for delivery + fintech partners worldwide

problemPersonal.title: Your city is already sharing rides. The system just has not caught up.
problemPersonal.subtitle: These are the things riders and drivers already live with. Tag Rides starts in Lagos because Lagos makes the problem impossible to ignore.

complaints.quote: I'm paying ₦5,000 to Bolt every morning so I don't have to sit in a danfo. That's ₦100,000 a month I don't have to lose.
I wait 20 minutes for a danfo to fill, then 90 minutes in traffic. By the time I get to work I'm already tired.
Surge pricing during rain. Surge pricing during traffic. Surge pricing because… reasons. There has to be a better way.
I drive to work every day with three empty seats. Three people on the road I'd happily pick up if I could trust them.

howItWorks.title: Three taps. One ride. Real savings.
howItWorks.subtitle: We took everything you already do — find a driver going your way, agree a fair price, hop in — and added the records, ratings, and trip visibility around it.

steps.title: Tell us where you're going
steps.detail: Type your destination. Tag Rides finds drivers already heading that way.

steps.title: Send the fare offer
steps.detail: Start with the system's draft. The driver accepts or counters once; you accept, decline, or leave the response open.
steps.hint: Danfo-level fare logic, with a trip record.

steps.title: Get in. Get there. Get on with it.
steps.detail: Verified driver. Trip tracked. Rate them when you arrive. Move on with your day.
steps.hint: Both sides rate each other; bad actors leave the network.

modes.title: Tag-Along first. Direct when you need the whole car.
modes.subtitle: The signature mode is Tag-Along — share the route, share the fare. Direct is there when privacy matters more than price.

modes.name: Tag-Along
modes.tagline: Share a ride with up to 3 others going your way. Pay only for your leg.
features: Danfo-level fare logic — drivers already going that direction
Driver picks the route, riders board along it
Driver accepts or counters once; rider chooses what stays open
No detours — you go a known path

modes.name: Direct
modes.tagline: The whole car. Door-to-door. When the trip is yours alone.
features: Driver routes to your destination directly
Same verified drivers; same rating system

safety.title: Six things that have to be true before you tap Request.
safety.subtitle: Shared rides become safer when the system is accountable. Every layer below has to hold for the next one to matter.

signals.title: Verified drivers
signals.detail: Government ID, vehicle details, and launch-market checks on file before activation.

signals.title: Two-sided ratings
signals.detail: Both riders and drivers rate every trip. One-star patterns lose access to the network.

signals.title: Live trip tracking
signals.detail: Share your live location with up to three trusted contacts during every ride.

signals.title: In-app emergency
signals.detail: One tap shares your live coordinates and trip details with the right safety contacts for that market.

signals.title: Match by track record
signals.detail: Riders see driver rating, completed trips, and verified-since date before accepting an offer.

signals.title: Trip log
signals.detail: Every ride is logged: route, fare, riders, driver, and time, so disputes have a record.

pricingFair.title: Danfo-level fares. Ride-hailing-level accountability.
pricingFair.subtitle: We ran the numbers on 30 actual Lagos journeys. The local fares are the proof; the route-share logic travels to other cities.

appCTA.title: Start with the first corridor.
appCTA.subtitle: We're starting at the heart of the Lagos commute — the Yaba ↔ Lagos Island corridor. Join the rider waitlist, or start driving if your daily route is already on the line.

competitive.title: Why TagRides works differently.
competitive.subtitle: Other services assign one car or sell one seat. Along lets riders send offers to several route-matched drivers, keep backups open, and confirm a seat only when they board.
competitive._note: This compares how each service handles a ride, not its overall quality. 'Partial' means the behavior is possible but not guaranteed.

rows: No driver detour for Along pickup
Offers sent to several matched drivers
Seat is confirmed when the rider boards

differentiation.line: Strong dispatch, but the driver makes a new pickup trip and the rider is tied to one driver before pickup.
Several driver prices help riders compare options, but choosing one closes every other option and sends a whole car to the pickup.
Monetises route-aligned seats, but booking removes inventory before physical pickup and alternatives do not remain live.
The closest offline comparison: fixed routes, shared seats, and payment on boarding. TagRides adds digital offers, backup options, identity, and trip records.

traction.title: Backed, built, and quietly accumulating users.
traction.subtitle: Live MVP. Real surveys. Capital and credits already secured.

proof.label: Waitlist signups
proof.context: Mostly along the Yaba commute belt

proof.label: Live MVP on AWS
proof.context: Flutter app, Go services, MQTT real-time

team.title: A solo operator who ships, backed by Lagos veterans.
team.subtitle: Full-stack execution from one founder. Advisory weight from people who actually move things in Lagos.

members.name: Oniya Olaiya
members.bio: Ships the full stack — Flutter rider + driver app, Go backend services, MQTT real-time geolocation pipeline, AWS infra. Built the production system independently. ALX Ventures alum; relocating from EC2 development to in-market Lagos lead.

members.name: Efemena Abraham
members.bio: Lagos tech-ecosystem operator. Apex Network introductions across logistics + payments + Lagos State partnerships.

members.name: Pst. Wale Olasoji
members.bio: Corporate operations + Lagos community gateway. Fuel-economics + driver-network access at scale.

financials.title: Profitable from year two on a 5-year run.
financials.subtitle: Conservative — driver supply is the rate-limit, not demand. Cohort retention modeled at 35% by month 3.

items.label: MVP live on AWS — Flutter + Go
Public Lagos launch — Yaba ↔ Lagos Island corridor
First non-African market — SE Asia / LATAM pilot

impact.title: Formalising the informal — and making cities work.
impact.subtitle: Tag Rides turns everyday drivers into verified entrepreneurs, digitising a $2B informal transport market.

outcomes.label: Replicable corridor playbook after Lagos proof
Informal transport category ready for digitisation
Empty seats earning without extra pickup detours

ask.title: Seeking $750K to make Lagos the proof.
ask.subtitle: Pre-seed on a SAFE. Eighteen months of runway. Non-dilutive backing already secured from Tony Elumelu Foundation, Ugwumba Enterprise Challenge, and $52K in cloud credits. We're now raising priced capital to launch the Yaba ↔ Lagos Island corridor, reach Seed-ready unit economics, and earn the right to do this in every city the math fails.
